description: A visual command center dashboard for Claude Code customizations - shows commands, agents, plugins, hooks, and skills at a glance

userStories.title: Project scaffolding with Next.js and shadcn/ui
userStories.description: Initialize the Next.js 14 project with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui component library
acceptanceCriteria: package.json exists with next, react, typescript dependencies
src/components/ui/ directory exists with at least Card component
npm run dev starts without errors
userStories.notes: Use 'npx create-next-app@latest' then 'npx shadcn@latest init'. Install Card, Badge, Button, Input, ScrollArea components.

userStories.title: Define TypeScript interfaces for all data models
userStories.description: Create type definitions for Command, Agent, Plugin, Hook, Skill, and ScanResponse
acceptanceCriteria: Command interface has: name, path, description, lastModified, scope, projectName?
Agent interface has: name, path, description, lastModified
Plugin interface has: name, version, enabled, installedAt, source
Hook interface has: name, path, type, lastModified
Skill interface has: name, path, description, lastModified
ScanResponse interface combines all with scannedAt timestamp
npm run build passes (no type errors)
userStories.notes: These interfaces mirror the PRD data model section exactly

userStories.title: Commands scanner service
userStories.description: Create a scanner that reads ~/.claude/commands/ and extracts command info including YAML frontmatter
acceptanceCriteria: Parses YAML frontmatter to extract description
userStories.notes: Use gray-matter package for YAML frontmatter parsing. Use fs and path from Node.js.

userStories.title: Agents scanner service
userStories.description: Create a scanner that reads ~/.claude/agents/ and extracts agent info
userStories.notes: Similar pattern to commands scanner but extracts first paragraph instead of frontmatter

userStories.title: Plugins scanner service
userStories.description: Create a scanner that reads plugin data from ~/.claude/plugins/ and settings.json
acceptanceCriteria: Returns array of Plugin objects with correct enabled boolean
Handles missing files gracefully (returns empty array)
userStories.notes: Cross-reference installed_plugins.json with settings.json to determine enabled status

userStories.title: Hooks scanner service
userStories.description: Create a scanner that reads ~/.claude/hooks/ directory
userStories.notes: .sh files are shell, .js/.ts are node, others are 'other'

userStories.title: Skills scanner service
userStories.description: Create a scanner that reads ~/.claude/skills/ directory
acceptanceCriteria: Extracts description from skill.md or README.md if present
userStories.notes: Skills are folders, not files. Look for description in metadata files.

userStories.title: Main scanner index and API route
userStories.description: Create index that combines all scanners and API route to expose scan results
acceptanceCriteria: scanAll() calls all 5 scanners and returns ScanResponse
GET /api/scan returns JSON with all scan results
userStories.notes: API route uses Next.js App Router format

userStories.title: Dashboard header component
userStories.description: Create the dashboard header with title and search bar
userStories.notes: Search functionality will be wired up later

userStories.title: ItemCard reusable component
userStories.description: Create a reusable card component for displaying any item type
acceptanceCriteria: Shows last modified as relative time (e.g., '2d ago')
Accepts icon prop for visual differentiation
userStories.notes: Use date-fns for relative time formatting

userStories.title: CommandList component
userStories.description: Create component that displays list of commands
userStories.notes: Follow the visual design from PRD wireframe

userStories.title: AgentList component
userStories.description: Create component that displays list of agents
acceptanceCriteria: Renders ItemCard for each agent with robot icon
userStories.notes: Use appropriate icon (robot emoji or Lucide icon)

userStories.title: PluginList component with status badges
userStories.description: Create component that displays plugins with enabled/disabled status
acceptanceCriteria: Shows section header 'PLUGINS' with enabled/total count
Uses Badge component for enabled (green) / disabled (gray) status
Shows checkmark or X icon based on status
userStories.notes: Count format like '7/8 on' in header

userStories.title: HookList component
userStories.description: Create component that displays hooks
userStories.notes: Hooks are automations that run on triggers

userStories.title: SkillList component
userStories.description: Create component that displays skills
userStories.notes: Skills are complex capabilities

userStories.title: Assemble main dashboard page
userStories.description: Wire up all components into the main page with data fetching
acceptanceCriteria: Renders all 5 list components with real data
Has Refresh button that re-fetches data
Page is visually organized per PRD wireframe
userStories.notes: Use React hooks for data fetching and state

userStories.title: Open in VS Code action
userStories.description: Add button to open any item's file in VS Code
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking button opens file path in VS Code
Uses 'code' CLI command via API route
userStories.notes: Use child_process.exec to run 'code <path>'

userStories.title: Search functionality
userStories.description: Wire up search to filter items across all categories
acceptanceCriteria: Typing in search bar filters displayed items
Search matches against name and description
Filtered counts update in section headers
userStories.notes: Client-side filtering, no API call needed

userStories.title: Polish visual design
userStories.description: Apply final styling to match PRD wireframe aesthetic
acceptanceCriteria: Color coding for badges (green enabled, gray disabled, blue global)
userStories.notes: Reference the PRD wireframe for exact visual targets

userStories.title: Launch script and documentation
userStories.description: Create one-command launcher and update README
acceptanceCriteria: Running ./scripts/start.sh launches dev server and opens browser
README.md explains what the project does
README.md includes setup and usage instructions
userStories.notes: Script should cd to project, run npm dev, wait, then open browser
